tiktok.caption: Your side hustle income gets taxed at 15.3% BEFORE income tax even touches it — and 45% of freelancers miss the quarterly deadline too.

youtube_short.title: Your Side Hustle Has a Hidden 15% Tax
title_alternatives: The Self-Employment Tax Nobody Warns You About
Why Your Side Hustle Is Secretly Taxed at 37%
youtube_short.description:
Most side hustlers only think about income tax — but there's a 15.3% self-employment tax that hits first, before income tax even touches your money. That's because at a regular job your employer pays half of your Social Security and Medicare. When you're self-employed, you pay both halves. On the average $8,000 in side hustle income, that's a $1,224 surprise bill — and if you miss quarterly payments, add penalties on top. The fix: deduct half the SE tax on your return, open a SEP-IRA, and set aside 30% of every payment the moment it lands. #personalfinance #sidehustle #taxes #freelance #moneytips

Follow for a new money trap exposed every day.

hook_line: Your side hustle income gets taxed TWICE — and most people find out too late
